meaning: adj.<英>数万亿的 <美>数十亿的
examples: An oil boom would also mean a multibillion dollar windfall in tax revenues, royalties and leasing fees for Alaska and the Federal Government.
An oil boom would also mean a multibillion-dollar windfall in tax revenues, royalties and leasing fees for Alaska and the Federal Government.
The housing project is hard to shove for attention among multibillion-pound infrastructure projects, so it is inevitable that the attention is focused elsewhere.
Such tiny changes might seem trifling in comparison with the multibillion-dollar nature of the problem.
Mark Spencer finishes his barbecue and resumes wreaking havoc on the multibillion-dollar phone equipment business.
